description: Immerse yourself in the vibrant culture and rich history of New Orleans, a city where jazz flows as freely as the Mississippi River. Over six unforgettable days, you and your partner will explore the soulful streets, indulge in mouthwatering cuisine, and discover the unique charm that makes this city a beloved destination. From the lively sounds of street musicians to the tantalizing aromas wafting from local eateries, every moment will be filled with the spirit of New Orleans. Get ready for a journey that celebrates your passions and creates lasting memories!

activities.description: Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast of beignets and café au lait at the iconic Café du Monde. The powdered sugar-dusted pastries are a must-try!

Stroll through the vibrant streets of the French Quarter, where you can admire the historic architecture and lively atmosphere. Don't forget to check out the local shops for unique souvenirs and jazz memorabilia!
Enjoy a casual lunch at Coop's Place, known for its delicious Cajun and Creole dishes. Try the jambalaya or the fried chicken for an authentic taste of New Orleans.

activities.activity: Visit the New Orleans Jazz Museum
activities.description: Dive into the rich history of jazz at the New Orleans Jazz Museum. Explore exhibits featuring legendary musicians and the evolution of jazz in the city. It's a perfect spot for jazz enthusiasts!
Head to local shops in the French Quarter to find unique jazz-related merchandise. Look for vinyl records, t-shirts, and other memorabilia to take home as a reminder of your trip.
Savor a hearty dinner at The Gumbo Shop, where you can indulge in a bowl of authentic gumbo or étouffée. The cozy atmosphere and flavorful dishes make it a great dining experience.
End your day with an unforgettable live jazz performance at Preservation Hall. This historic venue showcases some of the best local jazz musicians, providing an authentic New Orleans experience.
Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast of beignets and café au lait at the iconic Café du Monde. The powdered sugar-dusted pastries are a must-try!
Explore the vibrant Frenchmen Street, known for its live music scene. Check out local shops for unique jazz memorabilia and enjoy the lively atmosphere.
Enjoy a delicious lunch at The Praline Connection, famous for its Creole cuisine. Try the jambalaya or gumbo for an authentic taste of New Orleans.
Experience the heart of New Orleans jazz at Preservation Hall. Attend a live performance and immerse yourself in the rich musical heritage of the city.
Stroll through the nearby shops to find unique jazz-related merchandise, from vinyl records to T-shirts featuring your favorite artists.
Savor a casual dinner at Coop's Place, known for its laid-back vibe and delicious Cajun dishes. Don't miss their famous rabbit and sausage jambalaya!

activities.activity: Evening Jazz at The Spotted Cat Music Club
activities.description: End your day with live jazz at The Spotted Cat Music Club, a cozy venue that showcases local talent. Enjoy the intimate setting and great music.

Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast at the iconic Café du Monde. Indulge in their famous beignets, dusted with powdered sugar, and sip on a rich café au lait while soaking in the lively atmosphere of the French Quarter.

Stroll through the historic French Market, where you can find local crafts, fresh produce, and unique souvenirs. This vibrant market is perfect for picking up some jazz-themed memorabilia to take home.
Enjoy a delicious lunch at Cochon Butcher, known for its artisanal sandwiches and Cajun-inspired dishes. Try the muffuletta or the pork belly sandwich for a true taste of New Orleans.

activities.activity: Visit the New Orleans Jazz Museum
activities.description: Dive into the rich history of jazz at the New Orleans Jazz Museum. Explore exhibits featuring legendary musicians and the evolution of jazz music in the city. Don't forget to check out the gift shop for unique jazz-themed items.
Experience an authentic jazz performance at Preservation Hall, a historic venue dedicated to preserving New Orleans jazz. Enjoy the intimate setting and the incredible talent of local musicians.
Savor a hearty dinner at The Gumbo Shop, where you can enjoy traditional New Orleans dishes like gumbo and jambalaya. The cozy atmosphere and flavorful dishes make it a perfect end to your day.

Take a leisurely stroll down Frenchmen Street, known for its vibrant nightlife and live music scene. Stop by local bars and clubs to catch some live jazz performances and soak in the lively ambiance of the city.
Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast of beignets and café au lait at the iconic Café du Monde. The powdered sugar-dusted pastries are a must-try!
Explore the National WWII Museum, a fascinating and immersive experience that chronicles the American experience in the war. Don't miss the 4D movie that brings history to life!
Enjoy a delicious lunch at Cochon Butcher, known for its artisanal sandwiches and Cajun-inspired dishes. The house-made charcuterie is a highlight!
Take a leisurely stroll through the beautiful Garden District, famous for its stunning antebellum mansions and lush gardens. This area is perfect for some romantic photo opportunities!

activities.activity: Shopping for Jazz Merchandise at Frenchmen Street
activities.description: Head to Frenchmen Street, where you can find unique jazz merchandise and souvenirs. Explore local shops and support artists while picking up some great finds!
Savor authentic New Orleans cuisine at The Gumbo Shop, where you can enjoy a bowl of rich gumbo or jambalaya. The cozy atmosphere makes it a perfect dinner spot!

activities.activity: Live Jazz at The Spotted Cat Music Club
activities.description: End your day with live jazz music at The Spotted Cat Music Club, a beloved local venue. Immerse yourself in the vibrant atmosphere and enjoy the sounds of talented musicians!

Stroll along Magazine Street, known for its eclectic shops and boutiques. Here, you can find unique jazz merchandise and local art, perfect for souvenirs and gifts.
Enjoy a delicious lunch at Cochon Butcher, a casual eatery known for its artisanal sandwiches and Cajun specialties. Don't miss their house-made charcuterie!

Head to Frenchmen Street, where you can find local shops selling jazz-related merchandise, from vinyl records to unique memorabilia. Perfect for any jazz lover!
Savor a hearty dinner at The Praline Connection, famous for its Creole cuisine. Try their jambalaya or gumbo for an authentic taste of New Orleans.

activities.activity: Live Jazz at The Spotted Cat Music Club
activities.description: End your day with live jazz at The Spotted Cat Music Club, a cozy venue featuring local musicians. Enjoy the vibrant atmosphere and dance the night away!

departure.place: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport

transit.memo: Direct flight back to Washington D.C.

activities.description: Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast at the iconic Café du Monde. Enjoy their famous beignets dusted with powdered sugar and a café au lait to fuel your day of exploration.

activities.activity: Visit the New Orleans Jazz Museum
activities.description: Dive into the rich history of jazz at the New Orleans Jazz Museum. Located in the historic Old U.S. Mint, the museum showcases fascinating exhibits on jazz legends and the evolution of this vibrant music genre.

Savor some local Creole cuisine at Coop's Place, known for its laid-back atmosphere and delicious dishes like jambalaya and fried chicken. A perfect spot to experience authentic flavors before heading out.

activities.activity: Shopping for Jazz Merchandise at Frenchmen Street
activities.description: Stroll down Frenchmen Street, where you can find various shops selling jazz-related merchandise, from vinyl records to unique souvenirs. It's a great place to pick up some memorabilia to remember your trip.
